body.title: CLOSED: Design shared palace schema: palace.shared.* + palace.projects.*
body.result: palace_schema.md: palace.shared.* + palace.projects.* namespaces, node shape, bootstrap sequence, gating table. Commit f8cf3f6.
body.description:
The palace needs to be a shared agent-layer resource (not CC-private) serving all 4 consumers: Akien, CC, Igor, Rack-Minion. Design the schema as a markdown doc before any implementation begins — it gates SummarizerDevice, context-load redesign, and docs tree.

Proposed shape: palace.shared.akien (profile, working style), palace.shared.rules (coding, commits, memory, database, budget), palace.shared.capabilities (cross-agent skill/device inventory), palace.shared.audits (audit check registry). palace.projects.<name>.summary, palace.projects.<name>.map, palace.projects.<name>.standards, palace.projects.<name>.decisions. Igor's palace stays in TheIgors postgres — palace.projects.theigors contains a pointer node only (federate, don't merge).

Deliverables: docs/palace_schema.md in agent_datacenter, with one section per namespace, example node shapes, and a gating table showing what each downstream ticket needs from the schema.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/docs/palace_schema.md (new)
**Design rules:** No TheIgors imports; schema must work for all 4 consumers; ADC portable.
**Scope boundary:** Schema doc only — no DB creation, no migrations, no code. Out of scope: implementation of any schema node.
**Test plan:** No automated tests (design doc). Manual review by Akien before dependent tickets begin.
